description_long: This object consists of a group of weathered wooden crates, each constructed from rough, grayish-brown planks arranged in a classic cross-braced pattern. The crates vary in size, with some stacked atop one another and others arranged side by side, creating a sense of organized storage. The wood appears aged, with visible grain, knots, and slight discoloration, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements or heavy use. The surfaces are matte and slightly rough, with no visible handles, locks, or labeling. The crates are hollow, designed to store or transport goods, and their construction implies they can be opened, though no lids or closures are visible. This set would typically be found in urban environments such as warehouses, garages, alleys, or construction sites.
description: A group of weathered, gray wooden crates with cross-braced panels, stacked and arranged for storage or transport. The crates have a rough, aged appearance and are commonly found in urban storage or industrial settings.
description_view_0: Multiple wooden crates are stacked and grouped, showing the front and side cross-braced panels with visible wood grain.
description_view_1: A top-down view reveals the flat tops of the crates, highlighting their arrangement and the cross-bracing on the lids.
description_view_2: The crates are seen from the opposite side, displaying more of the vertical faces and the stacked configuration.
description_view_3: A rear-angled view shows the backs and tops of the crates, emphasizing their depth and the irregular stacking.
